message:
fix(disk): peel `/<drive>:/...` after `//` strip too (#93 follow-up)

Issue #93's URL parser fix used an `else if` chain so the drive-letter
peel never ran after the `//` strip.  That left `sqlite:////C:/path`
(an explicit four-slash absolute form some tooling emits) parsing to
`/C:/path` instead of `C:/path` — which Win32 file APIs reject.

Use separate `if` (not `else if`) so both trims compose.

Validated on Windows native: brings the disk module suite from 28/30 to
29/30 (the remaining failure is a pre-existing test that assumes Linux
semantics of `fs2::available_space` on a nonexistent root path).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
